title: Dna from cell-free medium
abstract: The present disclosure relates to methods and products for producing DNA of embryonic origin for analysis. In certain embodiments, the present disclosure provides a method of producing DNA of embryonic origin for analysis, the method comprising: obtaining a sample of cell-free medium comprising DNA in which an embryo was cultured, the embryo being at day 4 of development or greater; and amplifying the DNA to produce DNA of embryonic origin for analysis.
claims:
Claims 1. A method of producing DNA of embryonic origin for analysis, the method comprising: obtaining a sample of cell-free medium comprising DNA in which an embryo was cultured, the embryo being at day 4 of development or greater; and amplifying the DNA to produce DNA of embryonic origin for analysis. 2. The method according to claim 1, wherein the cell free medium is medium in which the embryo is at day 5 of development or greater. 3. The method according to claim 1, wherein the cell free medium is medium in which the embryo is at day 4 to day 6 of development. 4. The method according to claim 1, wherein the cell free medium is medium in which the embryo is at day 4 to day 5 of development. 5. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the mean size of DNA in the cell-free medium comprises a mean DNA size of 200 base pairs or greater. 6. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the largest DNA in the cell-free medium comprises a size of 220 base pairs or greater, 300 base pairs or greater, 400 base pairs or greater, 500 base pairs or greater, or 1000 base pairs or greater. 7. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the embryo has undergone assisted hatching to breach the zona pellucida. 8. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the method comprises exposure of the cell-free medium to a cell disruption treatment prior to and/or concurrently with amplifying the DNA. 9. The method according to claim 8, wherein the cell disruption treatment comprises exposure to one or more of a proteinase, a detergent one and heating. 10. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the method comprises enriching for exosomes in the cell-free medium prior to amplifying the DNA. 11. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the method comprises enriching and/or size selecting DNA from the cell-free medium prior to and/or after amplifying the DNA. 12. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 11, wherein the method comprises use of a sample of cell-free medium of 20 ul or less. 13. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein the method comprises use of a sample of cell-free medium of less than 10 ul. 14. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein the method comprises determining the amount of DNA produced after amplification. 15. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 14, wherein an amount of amplified DNA produced greater than 14 ng/uL is indicative that the DNA produced is suitable for analysis. 16. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 15, wherein the method is used to produce DNA for pre-implantation genetic testing. 17. A method of pre-implantation genetic testing of an embryo, the method comprising: obtaining a sample of cell-free medium comprising DNA in which an embryo was cultured, the embryo being at day 4 of development or greater; amplifying the DNA to produce DNA of embryonic origin for analysis; and assessing the DNA for pre-implantation genetic testing. 18. A method of producing DNA of embryonic origin for analysis, the method comprising: obtaining a sample of cell-free medium comprising DNA in which an embryo was cultured; amplifying the DNA; and assessing the size of the DNA prior to and/or after DNA amplification, wherein a mean size of DNA of greater than 200 base pairs and/or a largest DNA of a size of 220 base pairs or greater is indicative that the embryonic DNA is suitable for analysis. 19. The method according to claim 18, wherein the embryo has undergone assisted hatching to breech the zona pellucida. 20. The method according to claims 18 or 19, wherein the method comprises exposure of the cell-free medium to a cell disruption treatment prior to and/or concurrently with amplifying the DNA. 21. The method according to claim 20, wherein the cell disruption treatment comprises exposure to one or more of a proteinase, a detergent and heating. 22. The method according to any one of claims 18 to 21, wherein the method comprises enriching for exosomes in the cell-free medium prior to amplifying the DNA. 23. The method according to any one of claims 18 to 22, wherein the method comprises enriching and/or size selecting the DNA from the cell-free medium prior to and/or following amplifying the DNA. 24. The method according to any one of claims 18 to 23, wherein the method comprises use of a sample of cell-free medium of 20 ul or less. 25. The method according to any one of claims 18 to 24, wherein the method comprises use of a sample of cell-free medium of less than 10 ul. 26. The method according to any one of claims 18 to 25, wherein the method comprises determining the amount of DNA produced after amplification. 27. The method according to any one of claims 18 to 26, wherein an amount of amplified DNA produced greater than 14 ng/uL is indicative that the embryonic DNA produced is suitable for analysis. 28. The method according to any one of claims 18 to 27, wherein the cell free medium is medium in which the embryo is at day 5 of development or greater. 29. The method according to any one of claims 18 to 27, wherein the cell free medium is medium in which the embryo is at day 4 to day 6 of development. 30. The method according to any one of claims 18 to 27, wherein the cell free medium is medium in which the embryo is at day 4 to day 5 of development. 31. The method according to any one of claims 18 to 30, wherein the method is used to produce DNA for pre-implantation genetic testing. 32. A method of pre-implantation genetic testing of an embryo, the method comprising: obtaining a sample of cell-free medium comprising DNA in which the embryo was cultured; amplifying the DNA to produce DNA of embryonic origin for analysis; and assessing the DNA prior to and/or after amplification for pre-implantation genetic testing, wherein a mean size of DNA of greater than 200 base pairs and/or a largest DNA of a size of greater than 220 base pairs is indicative that the embryonic DNA is suitable for pre-implantation genetic screening. 33. A method of producing DNA of embryonic origin for analysis, the method comprising: obtaining a sample of cell-free medium comprising DNA in which an embryo was cultured; amplifying the DNA; and 

 size selecting DNA prior to and/or after amplifying the DNA to produce DNA of embryonic origin for analysis, wherein the size selection comprises selection for DNA having a mean size of greater than 220 base pairs. 34. A method of producing DNA of embryonic origin for analysis, the method comprising: obtaining a sample of cell-free medium in which an embryo was cultured; enriching for exosomes present in the cell-free medium; and amplifying DNA in the exosome enriched sample to produce DNA of embryonic origin for analysis. 35. The method according to claim 34, wherein the embryo has undergone assisted hatching to breech the zona pellucida. 36. The method according to claims 34 or 35, wherein the method comprises immunoaffmity to enrich the exosomes. 37. The method according to any one of claims 34 to 36, wherein the method comprises use of a sample of cell-free medium of 20 ul or less. 38. The method according to any one of claims 34 to 37, wherein the method comprises use of a sample of cell-free medium of less than 10 ul. 39. The method according to any one of claims 34 to 38, wherein the cell free medium is medium in which the embryo is at day 5 of development or greater. 40. The method according to any one of claims 34 to 38, wherein the cell free medium is medium in which the embryo is at day 4 to day 6 of development. 41. The method according to any one of claims 34 to 38, wherein the cell free medium is medium in which the embryo is at day 4 to day 5 of development. 42. The method according to any one of claims 34 to 41, wherein the method comprises determining the amount of embryonic DNA produced after amplifying the DNA. 43. The method according to any one of claims 34 to 42, wherein an amount of amplified DNA produced greater than 14 ng/uL is indicative that the embryonic DNA produced is suitable for analysis. 44. The method according to any one of claims 33 to 42, wherein the method is used to produce DNA of embryonic origin for pre-implantation genetic testing. 45. A method of pre-implantation genetic testing of an embryo, the method comprising: obtaining a sample of cell-free medium in which the embryo was cultured; enriching for exosomes present in the cell-free medium; and amplifying DNA in the exosome enriched sample to produce DNA of embryonic origin for analysis. 46. DNA produced by the method according to any one of claims 1 to 15, 18 to 31, and 33 to 43. 47. A method of pre-implantation genetic testing, the method comprising analysing DNA according to claim 46. 48. Use of embryonic exosomes for producing DNA of embryonic origin for analysis. 49. Use of embryonic exosomes for preimplantation genetic testing. 50. A kit for performing the method according to any one of claims 1 to 46.
